zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Saint Quintin於1233年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾馬福利特時，她的父親Alexander De Saint Quintin18歲，母親Margaret Albini23歲。她於大約1257年與Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1262年享年29歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Saint Quintin于1233年出生在英国英格兰越可爾馬福利特时，她的父亲Alexander De Saint Quintin18岁，母亲Margaret Albini23岁。她于大约1257年与Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1262年享年29岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Quintin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Yves, Andre, Dominique, Gilles, Lucien, Michel, Oliva, Roch.
Breton: from the personal name Quintin, which is of the same Latin origin as 2 below, but also considered a derivative of Old Breton kint ‘first’.
French; Spanish (Quintín): from the personal name Quintin (Spanish Quintín), from Latin Quintinus, a Christian saint's name (see Quentin ).

variants.name: Quinton
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Quentin, Quintin (Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, and brought to England by the Normans. The surname was altered to Quinton by association with the placenames in 2 below.

English: habitational name from from any of three places called Quinton (Gloucestershire, Northamptonshire, Worcestershire) or from Quainton (Buckinghamshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English cwēn ‘queen’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, or perhaps from Old English cwene ‘woman’ + tūn or an Old English personal name Cwēna + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Quentin (Manche) in France or possibly from Saint-Quentin-en-Tourmont (Somme), the site of the martyrdom of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. It is not certain that this surname survived into the modern period.

variants.name: Quentin
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Quentin (from Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. Compare Quintin .
English: variant of   Quinton  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Saint Quintin was born in 1233, in Marfleet, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Alexander De Saint Quintin, was 18 and her mother, Margaret Albini, was 23. She married Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet about 1257, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1262, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Saint Quintin est née en 1233, à Marfleet, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alexander De Saint Quintin, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margaret Albini, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet environ 1257, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1262, à l’âge de 29 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Saint Quintin 1233 in Marfleet, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alexander De Saint Quintin 18 und ihre Mutter Margaret Albini 23. Sie hat um 1257 in England, United Kingdom Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1262 im Alter von 29 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Saint Quintin è nata il nel 1233 nel Marfleet, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Alexander De Saint Quintin, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Margaret Albini, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet circa 1257, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1262, all'età di 29 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Saint Quintinが1233年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアマルフリートで生まれた時，父Alexander De Saint Quintinは18歳，母Margaret Albiniは23歳であった。約1257年にイギリスイギリスでStephen de Thorpe III de Merfletと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1262年に29歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Saint Quintin(이)가 1233년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 마플릿에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alexander De Saint Quintin(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Albini(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1257년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1262년 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Saint Quintin nasceu em 1233, em Marfleet, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Alexander De Saint Quintin, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Albini, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet aproximadamente 1257, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1262, com 29 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Saint Quintin родилась в 1233 г. в местности марфлит, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Alexander De Saint Quintin было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Albini, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet около 1257 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1262 г. в возрасте 29 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Saint Quintin nació en 1233, en Marfleet, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Alexander De Saint Quintin, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Margaret Albini, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Stephen de Thorpe III de Merflet aproximadamente 1257, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1262, a la edad de 29 años.
